name: Amazon S3
summary: Production monitoring, observability, and security adoption pack for S3 buckets and data access.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Observability
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Track request volume, errors, latency, storage growth, lifecycle transitions, and access patterns.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Explainability
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Use CloudTrail data events, access logs, and Storage Lens to explain who accessed what and when.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Quality
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Measure availability, request success, replication health, and lifecycle effectiveness.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Ethics and Safety
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Protect sensitive data using block public access, encryption, Macie, and least-privilege policies.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Continuous Validation
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Continuously validate bucket policy posture, encryption, public access, and data protection controls.

dashboard.name: S3 Data Protection and Access Health

sections.name: Bucket Overview
sections.purpose: Storage size, object count, growth trend, lifecycle activity, and replication posture.

sections.name: Request Health
sections.purpose: All requests, 4xx errors, 5xx errors, first-byte latency, and total request latency.

sections.name: Access and Audit
sections.purpose: CloudTrail data events, access log patterns, denied requests, and unusual principals.

sections.name: Data Protection
sections.purpose: Encryption, versioning, replication, object lock, and backup readiness.

sections.name: Sensitive Data
sections.purpose: Macie findings, sensitive data discovery status, and policy exceptions.

sections.name: Security Posture
sections.purpose: Block public access status, bucket policy risk, Config rules, and Security Hub findings.

metrics.name: BucketSizeBytes
metrics.why: Track storage growth and cost trend.

metrics.name: NumberOfObjects
metrics.why: Track object growth and lifecycle behavior.

metrics.name: AllRequests
metrics.why: Measure access volume and workload pattern.

metrics.name: 4xxErrors
metrics.why: Detect client authorization or request issues.

log_queries.name: S3 denied access events
log_queries.intent: Find denied S3 API calls in CloudTrail logs.
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, eventName, userIdentity.arn, requestParameters.bucketName, errorCode | filter eventSource = "s3.amazonaws.com" and ispresent(errorCode) | sort @timestamp desc | limit 50

log_queries.name: S3 delete operations
log_queries.intent: Find object or bucket delete events.
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, eventName, userIdentity.arn, requestParameters.bucketName, requestParameters.key | filter eventSource = "s3.amazonaws.com" and eventName like /Delete/ | sort @timestamp desc | limit 50

log_queries.name: S3 public policy changes
log_queries.intent: Find bucket policy or public access block changes.
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, eventName, userIdentity.arn, requestParameters.bucketName | filter eventSource = "s3.amazonaws.com" and eventName like /PutBucketPolicy|PutPublicAccessBlock|DeletePublicAccessBlock/ | sort @timestamp desc | limit 50

alarms.name: S3High4xxErrors
alarms.action: Review application credentials, bucket policy, object keys, and access patterns.

alarms.name: S3High5xxErrors
alarms.action: Check regional service health, retry behavior, and critical workflows.

alarms.name: S3LatencyHigh
alarms.action: Review object size, request path, client region, and transfer pattern.

alarms.name: S3PublicAccessChange
alarms.condition: CloudTrail detects public access block or bucket policy changes
alarms.action: Trigger security review and validate expected change ticket.

security_controls.name: Block public access
security_controls.evidence: Account and bucket-level block public access settings are enabled unless formally excepted.

security_controls.name: Default encryption
security_controls.evidence: Bucket default encryption is enabled with SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS.

security_controls.name: CloudTrail data events
security_controls.evidence: Critical buckets have object-level data events enabled for read/write audit.

security_controls.name: Sensitive data discovery
security_controls.evidence: Macie is enabled for sensitive buckets and findings are reviewed.

security_controls.name: Least-privilege access
security_controls.evidence: Bucket policies and IAM policies are scoped to required principals, actions, and prefixes.

adoption_plan: Classify buckets by criticality and data sensitivity.
Enable request metrics for critical buckets.
Enable CloudTrail data events for sensitive or regulated buckets.
Create dashboards for request health, latency, storage growth, and security posture.
Enable Macie for sensitive data discovery where required.
Create policy-change alarms and security review workflow.
Promote the pack as the default S3 data protection standard.
